---
title: "ggpaintr Manual Test Workbook"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

This workbook is the canonical interactive manual test guide for the current `ggpaintr` workflow. It is intended to complement, not replace, the package-standard automated tests in `tests/testthat/`.

It aligns with:

-   `.claude/specs/testing-strategy.md`
-   `tests/manual/manual-checklist-ggpaintr.md`
-   `tests/manual/ggpaintr-edge-cases.Rmd`

Current automated coverage for runtime feedback and the copy-rule system already includes:

-   malformed `expr` handling in `ptr_complete_expr_safe()`
-   invalid upload handling in `ptr_complete_expr_safe()`
-   plot-stage missing-object handling in `ptr_assemble_plot_safe()`
-   successful runtime assembly in `ptr_exec()`
-   exported app wiring for `ptr_exec()` and `outputError`
-   copy-rule validation, fallback resolution, and alias handling
-   default and overridden copy for app-shell controls and exported apps
-   custom Shiny integration helpers with custom top-level ids
-   custom placeholder registry parsing, runtime replacement, and export wiring
-   sourced exported-app smoke tests for static-data and upload-backed formulas
-   low-level runtime input discovery through `ptr_runtime_input_spec()`

See:

-   `tests/testthat/test-runtime-feedback.R`
-   `tests/testthat/test-export-shiny.R`
-   `tests/testthat/test-copy-rules.R`
-   `tests/testthat/test-extensibility.R`
-   `tests/testthat/test-placeholder-registry.R`
-   `tests/testthat/test-runtime-input-spec.R`

## Coverage Map

This workbook is intended to cover the maintained `ggpaintr` surface end to end.
Use the sections below as the canonical manual sweep for:

-   core app flow, plot updates, generated code, and layer toggles
-   built-in placeholders: `var`, `text`, `num`, `expr`, and `upload`
-   runtime error feedback, recovery, and plot-stage failure behavior
-   default copy rules and targeted `ui_text` overrides
-   exported app behavior for default and customized apps
-   embedded `ptr_server_state()` / bind-helper integrations
-   custom plot rendering with `ptr_extract_plot()`
-   custom placeholder registration and exported custom-placeholder apps
-   companion edge-case follow-up in `tests/manual/ggpaintr-edge-cases.Rmd`

## How To Use This Workbook

Use this file from an interactive RStudio session.

1.  Run the setup chunk first.
2.  Review the printed fixture paths.
3.  Run one app chunk at a time to launch `ptr_app()`.
4.  Close the current app before launching the next one.
5.  Use the expected-check lists below to record pass/fail behavior.

## Error Feedback Feature Test Guide

Use the scenarios below to test the dedicated inline error channel end to end.

| Scenario | Trigger | Expected error label | Plot behavior | Code behavior |
|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|
| Completion-stage error | malformed `expr` such as `~` | `Input error:` | plot clears / stays empty | code stays blank |
| Upload validation error | upload `bad_extension.txt` | `Input error:` | plot stays empty | code stays blank |
| Plot-stage error | missing object such as `unknown_object` | `Plot error:` | plot clears / stays empty | code remains visible |
| Recovery after error | correct the inputs and redraw | no error panel | valid plot renders | code updates normally |
| Exported app error behavior | run exported app and trigger one error case | same inline error as in-repo app | same clearing behavior | same code behavior |

## Setup

Assumptions:

-   Run this from the repo root.
-   Use the maintained package path centered on `R/paintr-app.R`, `R/paintr-parse.R`, `R/paintr-runtime.R`, `R/paintr-ui.R`, and `R/paintr-upload.R`.
-   Use the committed fixture files under `tests/testthat/fixtures/` for upload checks.

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggpaintr)
library(shiny)
library(shinyWidgets)
library(rlang)
library(assertthat)

csv_fixture <- file.path("tests", "testthat", "fixtures", "simple_numeric.csv")
rds_fixture <- file.path("tests", "testthat", "fixtures", "simple_numeric.rds")
bad_fixture <- file.path("tests", "testthat", "fixtures", "bad_extension.txt")

sales_manual <- data.frame(
  day = as.Date("2024-01-01") + 0:5,
  value = c(10, 13, 12, 16, 18, 17),
  segment = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C", "C")
)

spaced_manual <- data.frame(left = 1:4, right = c(2, 4, 6, 8), check.names = FALSE)
names(spaced_manual) <- c("first column", "second column")
spaced_manual_clean <- ptr_normalize_column_names(spaced_manual)

date_placeholder <- ptr_define_placeholder(
  keyword = "date",
  build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {
    shiny::dateInput(id, copy$label)
  },
  resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {
    if (is.null(value) || identical(as.character(value), "")) {
      return(ptr_missing_expr())
    }

    rlang::expr(as.Date(!!as.character(value)))
  },
  copy_defaults = list(label = "Choose a date for {param}")
)

date_placeholders <- ptr_merge_placeholders(
  list(date = date_placeholder)
)

date_ui_text <- list(
  defaults = list(
    date = list(label = "Pick any date")
  ),
  params = list(
    xintercept = list(
      date = list(label = "Reference date")
    )
  ),
  layers = list(
    geom_vline = list(
      date = list(
        xintercept = list(
          help = "Choose a cutoff date for the vertical guide."
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

manual_ids <- ptr_build_ids(
  control_panel = "builder_controls",
  draw_button = "render_plot",
  export_button = "export_app",
  plot_output = "main_plot",
  error_output = "main_error",
  code_output = "main_code"
)

list(
  csv_fixture = csv_fixture,
  rds_fixture = rds_fixture,
  bad_fixture = bad_fixture
)
```

Upload fixture paths to use during manual testing:

-   `r csv_fixture`
-   `r rds_fixture`
-   `r bad_fixture`

Recommended RStudio flow:

-   restart R if you have an older `ggpaintr` namespace loaded
-   run the setup chunk
-   launch one app chunk
-   perform the listed checks
-   close the app window before continuing

Use `tests/manual/ggpaintr-edge-cases.Rmd` for known unresolved boundaries that
need repro code or follow-up testing rather than ordinary pass/fail workbook
checks.

Low-level developer note:

-   If you are manually probing `ptr_exec()`, discover its input ids
    with `ptr_runtime_input_spec()` instead of hand-authoring raw ids.
-   Raw ids such as `ggplot+3+2` may still appear in printed output, but they
    are implementation details rather than the recommended documentation path.

## Formula 1: Static All-Placeholders Interaction

This app is the main static-data interaction sweep. It exercises `var`, `text`, `num`, `expr`, `outputCode`, and layer checkbox behavior in one place.

```{r formula-1, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = var), size = num, alpha = num) +
  labs(title = text, x = text, y = text) +
  facet_wrap(expr) +
  theme(legend.position = text)
")
```

Suggested manual inputs:

-   `x = Sepal.Length`
-   `y = Sepal.Width`
-   `color = Species`
-   `size = 2.5`
-   `alpha = 0.7`
-   `title = Iris manual test`
-   `x text = Sepal length`
-   `y text = Sepal width`
-   `facet_wrap expr = ~ Species`
-   `legend.position text = bottom`

Expected checks:

-   Control tabs render for all layers.
-   Clicking `draw` updates both the plot and `outputCode`.
-   Clearing `title` removes `labs(title = ...)` content from the generated code.
-   Clearing `facet_wrap` removes the faceting call from the generated code.
-   Clearing `legend.position` removes that theme argument from the generated code.
-   Unchecking `geom_point`, `labs`, `facet_wrap`, or `theme` removes that layer from both the plot and the code.

## Formula 2: `var` Picker Boundary

This app checks the shipped UI behavior for `var`. The default control is a
single-column picker backed by the current dataset, not a free-text expression
editor. Derived mappings belong in the formula text around `var`, not in the
picker input itself.

```{r formula-2, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = text)
")
```

Suggested manual inputs:

-   open the `x` and `y` pickers and inspect the available choices
-   `x = mpg`
-   `y = disp`
-   `title = var picker boundary`

Expected checks:

-   Each `var` control shows dataset column names instead of a free-text box.
-   Arbitrary expressions such as `log(mpg)` or `disp + 1` are not offered by
    the shipped control.
-   The plot draws successfully with column selections.
-   `outputCode` reflects the selected columns.

## Formula 2B: Formula-Level `var` Transforms

This app checks the supported derived-mapping workflow for `var`. The picker
still supplies column names, while the formula carries the transformation.

```{r formula-2c, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var + 1, y = log(var))) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = text)
")
```

Suggested manual inputs:

-   `x = mpg`
-   `y = disp`
-   `title = transformed var mapping`

Expected checks:

-   The `x` picker still offers only column names such as `mpg`.
-   The `y` picker still offers only column names such as `disp`.
-   The plot draws successfully after substituting those columns into the
    formula-level transforms.
-   `outputCode` shows expressions such as `mpg + 1` and `log(disp)`.

## Formula 2C: Local Column Normalization

This app checks the supported local-data workflow for non-syntactic column
names. Normalize the data first, then let the shipped `var` picker expose the
cleaned names.

```{r formula-2b, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = spaced_manual_clean, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = text)
")
```

Suggested manual inputs:

-   `x = first_column`
-   `y = second_column`
-   `title = normalized local data`

Expected checks:

-   The `var` controls show `first_column` and `second_column`.
-   The plot draws successfully with the normalized local object.
-   `outputCode` uses the normalized column names.

## Formula 3: Global Upload

This app checks upload-backed global data, delayed `var` controls, default/custom dataset names, and unsupported file handling.

```{r formula-3, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = upload, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(size = num) +
  labs(title = 'good')
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Launch the app and confirm the `var` selectors are not available before an upload.
-   Upload `simple_numeric.csv` from `tests/testthat/fixtures/simple_numeric.csv`.
-   Leave the dataset object name blank the first time.
-   Use `x = x`, `y = y`, `size = 2`, `title = uploaded scatter`.
-   Click `draw`.
-   Repeat with `simple_numeric.rds` from `tests/testthat/fixtures/simple_numeric.rds`.
-   Repeat again with custom dataset object name `manual_upload_data`.
-   Retry with `bad_extension.txt` from `tests/testthat/fixtures/bad_extension.txt`.

Expected checks:

-   `var` selectors appear only after a valid upload.
-   With a blank dataset name, the generated code uses a filename-derived object name.
-   With a custom dataset name, the generated code uses `manual_upload_data`.
-   The app draws successfully for both `.csv` and `.rds`.
-   Uploaded column choices stay normalized before they reach the `var` pickers.
-   The bad-extension upload shows a clear inline error without crashing the session.
-   When upload completion fails, the plot does not render.

## Formula 4: Layer-Specific Upload

This app checks mixed static/upload layering and confirms that upload-backed `var` controls apply only to the upload-backed layer.

```{r formula-4, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = var), size = 2) +
  geom_point(data = upload, aes(x = var, y = var, color = var), size = num) +
  labs(title = text)
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Launch the app and inspect the controls before upload.
-   Confirm the static `iris` layer does not need upload-driven controls.
-   Upload `simple_numeric.csv` or `simple_numeric.rds`.
-   Set upload-layer `x = x`, `y = y`, `color = group`, `size = 3`.
-   Set `title = mixed static/upload plot`.
-   Click `draw`.
-   Uncheck the upload-backed `geom_point` layer and draw again.

Expected checks:

-   Only the upload-backed layer depends on uploaded columns.
-   The static `iris` layer remains intact before and after upload.
-   The combined plot shows both static and uploaded layers when enabled.
-   Disabling the upload-backed layer leaves a valid static plot.

## Formula 5: Export Smoke Test

This app is the static export-oriented formula. Use it along with Formula 3 for the two export checks.

```{r formula-5, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(size = num) +
  labs(title = text, subtitle = text) +
  facet_grid(expr, labeller = expr)
")
```

Suggested manual inputs:

-   `x = Sepal.Length`
-   `y = Sepal.Width`
-   `row facet = Species`
-   `col facet = Species`
-   `labeller expr = label_both`
-   `size = 1.5`
-   `title = export test`
-   `subtitle = static formula export`

Expected checks:

-   The static app draws and updates `outputCode` correctly.
-   Export the generated app from this formula and run it.
-   Export the generated app from Formula 3 and run it.
-   Confirm the exported script shows explicit `ui <- ...`, `server <- function(...)`, and `shinyApp(ui, server)`.
-   Confirm the exported server assigns `ptr_state <- ptr_server(...)`.
-   In the exported upload app, repeat one CSV upload flow and one RDS upload flow.
-   Confirm the exported apps still render both the plot and the generated code.
-   Confirm exported apps also show the same inline error message behavior on bad `expr` or bad upload input.
-   Add one small custom output or observer that reads `ptr_state$runtime()` and confirm the `draw` workflow still behaves normally.

## Formula 6: Error Feedback Checks

Use these short apps to confirm the new draw-time error path for malformed expressions, invalid uploads, missing objects, and recovery after failure.

```{r formula-6a, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(expr)
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Enter `~` for `facet_wrap expr`.
-   Click `draw`.
-   Replace it with `~ Speciesasdf`.
-   Click `draw` again.

Expected checks:

-   The app shows an inline `Input error:` message for bare `~`.
-   The session keeps running.
-   No stale plot is rendered.
-   `outputCode` stays blank because expression completion failed.
-   With `~ Speciesasdf`, the inline error channel shows a `Plot error:` message mentioning `Speciesasdf`.
-   The plot pane does not show raw Shiny `Error: [object Object]`.
-   `outputCode` remains visible for the `~ Speciesasdf` case because completion succeeded before render-time failure.

```{r formula-6-upload-error, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = upload, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point()
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Launch the app.
-   Upload `bad_extension.txt`.
-   Click `draw`.

Expected checks:

-   The app shows an inline `Input error:` message.
-   Upload-backed `var` selectors do not become usable from the invalid file.
-   The plot stays empty.
-   `outputCode` stays blank.

```{r formula-6b, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = unknown_object, aes(x = mpg, y = disp)) +
  geom_point()
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Launch the app.
-   Click `draw`.

Expected checks:

-   The app launches without crashing before interaction.
-   The app shows an inline `Plot error:` message mentioning `unknown_object`.
-   The plot stays empty.
-   The generated code remains visible even though plot construction failed.

```{r formula-6c-recovery, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(expr)
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   First enter `~` for `facet_wrap expr` and click `draw`.
-   Confirm the inline `Input error:` appears and the plot stays empty.
-   Replace the input with `~ Species`.
-   Click `draw` again.

Expected checks:

-   The previous error message disappears after the successful draw.
-   The plot renders normally after recovery.
-   `outputCode` updates to the valid faceting code.
-   The session remains usable without restart.

## Runtime Assembly Edge Cases

Use these checks to confirm that runtime layer pruning still produces stable
plot assembly.

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Re-run Formula 1.
-   Uncheck `geom_point`, `labs`, `facet_wrap`, and `theme`.
-   Click `draw`.
-   Re-run Formula 3 or Formula 4.
-   Disable every optional non-`ggplot` layer and click `draw` again.

Expected checks:

-   A base `ggplot(...)` call with every optional layer unchecked still renders
    as a valid blank plot instead of showing an inline error.
-   `outputCode` keeps only the retained `ggplot(...)` call after those layers
    are removed.
-   The app remains responsive after toggling layers back on and drawing again.
-   There is no user-facing workflow in the shipped app that intentionally
    produces a truly empty plot-expression list; keep that internal runtime path
    covered through automated tests and the companion notes in
    `tests/manual/ggpaintr-edge-cases.Rmd`.

## Exported App Error Feedback Check

Use Formula 6A or the upload-error app above, then export the generated app and run it.

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Export the app after opening it in the workbook.
-   Inspect the exported script and confirm it defines `ui_text <- NULL`.
-   Confirm the exported script resolves shell labels with `ptr_resolve_ui_text(..., ui_text = ui_text)`.
-   Run the exported app.
-   Trigger one malformed `expr` error or one invalid upload error.
-   Correct the input and redraw.

Expected checks:

-   The exported app shows the same inline error message pattern as the in-repo app.
-   The default exported script exposes `ui_text <- NULL` instead of embedding the full default copy registry.
-   The plot clears on failure and renders again after recovery.
-   `outputCode` stays blank for completion-stage errors and updates normally after recovery.

## Formula 7: Default Copy Rules

This app checks the shipped default copy from the internal registry. It covers shell text, upload prompts, friendly unnamed `expr` copy, and plain-language hints.

```{r formula-7-default-copy, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = upload, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(size = num, alpha = num) +
  labs(title = text) +
  facet_grid(expr)
")
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Launch the app and confirm the title is `ggpaintr Plot Builder`.
-   Confirm the primary buttons read `Update plot` and `Export Shiny app`.
-   Confirm the upload controls read `Choose a data file` and `Optional dataset name`.
-   Confirm the upload help text mentions accepted `.csv` and `.rds` formats and blank-name behavior.
-   Upload `simple_numeric.csv`.
-   Confirm the `var` prompts read `Choose the x-axis column` and `Choose the y-axis column`.
-   Confirm the numeric prompts read `Point size` and `Transparency`.
-   Confirm the `Transparency` help text mentions values between `0` and `1`.
-   Confirm the title prompt reads `Plot title`.
-   Confirm the faceting prompt reads `Facet by` with placeholder `~ Species`.
-   Confirm no control label includes parser-style text such as `argument 1`.
-   Use `x = x`, `y = y`, `size = 2`, `alpha = 0.8`, `title = Default copy test`, `facet = ~ group`, then click `Update plot`.

Expected checks:

-   The shipped default copy appears across the shell, upload controls, numeric hints, and faceting prompt.
-   The app never surfaces raw parser labels such as `argument 1`.
-   The plot renders successfully after upload and `outputCode` updates normally.
-   The generated code still reflects the formula inputs rather than the UI wording.

## Formula 8: Custom Copy Rules

This app checks runtime copy overrides passed through `ui_text`.

```{r formula-8-custom-copy, eval=FALSE}
custom_ui_text <- list(
  shell = list(
    title = list(label = "Exploratory Plot Builder"),
    draw_button = list(label = "Render plot")
  ),
  params = list(
    x = list(var = list(label = "Pick the field for the x-axis")),
    y = list(var = list(label = "Pick the field for the y-axis")),
    title = list(text = list(label = "Chart title"))
  ),
  layers = list(
    facet_wrap = list(
      expr = list(
        `__unnamed__` = list(
          label = "Split the plot by",
          placeholder = "~ Species"
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

ptr_app(
  "
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = text) +
  facet_wrap(expr)
",
  ui_text = custom_ui_text
)
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Launch the app and confirm the title is `Exploratory Plot Builder`.
-   Confirm the draw button reads `Render plot`.
-   Confirm the `var` prompts read `Pick the field for the x-axis` and `Pick the field for the y-axis`.
-   Confirm the title prompt reads `Chart title`.
-   Confirm the faceting prompt reads `Split the plot by` with placeholder `~ Species`.
-   Confirm the export button still reads `Export Shiny app`.
-   Confirm layer checkboxes still read `Include this layer in the plot`.
-   Use `x = Sepal.Length`, `y = Sepal.Width`, `title = Custom copy test`, `facet = ~ Species`, then click `Render plot`.

Expected checks:

-   The targeted prompts use the custom wording from `ui_text`.
-   Untargeted controls continue to use the package defaults.
-   Plot rendering and generated code behavior remain unchanged apart from the visible UI text.

## Formula 9: Exported Custom Copy Rules

This export check confirms that a generated app carries only compact custom overrides into the exported script and preserves the same custom wording at runtime.

```{r formula-9-exported-custom-copy, eval=FALSE}
custom_ui_text <- list(
  shell = list(
    title = list(label = "Exploratory Plot Builder"),
    draw_button = list(label = "Render plot")
  ),
  params = list(
    x = list(var = list(label = "Pick the field for the x-axis")),
    y = list(var = list(label = "Pick the field for the y-axis")),
    title = list(text = list(label = "Chart title"))
  ),
  layers = list(
    facet_wrap = list(
      expr = list(
        `__unnamed__` = list(
          label = "Split the plot by",
          placeholder = "~ Species"
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

custom_formula <- "
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = text) +
  facet_wrap(expr)
"

custom_obj <- ptr_parse_formula(custom_formula)
custom_out_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".R")

ptr_generate_shiny(
  custom_obj,
  custom_out_file,
  style = FALSE,
  ui_text = custom_ui_text
)

custom_out_file
source(custom_out_file)
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Run the chunk and note the generated file path.
-   Inspect the exported script and confirm it defines `custom_ui_text <- ...`.
-   Confirm the exported script rebuilds `ui_text` with `ptr_merge_ui_text(custom_ui_text)`.
-   Confirm the default export case would instead define `ui_text <- NULL`.
-   Confirm the exported server calls `ptr_server(..., ui_text = ui_text)`.
-   Run the exported app and confirm the title is `Exploratory Plot Builder`.
-   Confirm the draw button reads `Render plot`.
-   Confirm the overridden `x`, `y`, `title`, and faceting prompts match Formula 8.
-   Confirm untargeted controls, such as the export button and layer checkboxes, still use the default wording.
-   Use `x = Sepal.Length`, `y = Sepal.Width`, `title = Exported custom copy`, `facet = ~ Species`, then render the plot.

Expected checks:

-   The exported app preserves the same custom wording as the live app.
-   The exported script carries a compact local `custom_ui_text` object and rebuilds `ui_text` from it before calling the package runtime.
-   The custom export keeps `custom_ui_text <- ...`, while the default export keeps `ui_text <- NULL`.
-   Plot rendering and generated code continue to work normally in the exported app.

## Formula 10: Embedded App with Custom Ids and Bind Helpers

This app checks the supported phase-1 integration surface: `ptr_build_ids()`,
`ptr_server_state()`, and the `ptr_register_*()` helpers.

```{r formula-10-custom-ids, eval=FALSE}
ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Embedded ggpaintr manual test"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      ptr_input_ui(ids = manual_ids),
      tags$hr(),
      textOutput("runtimeStatus")
    ),
    mainPanel(
      ptr_output_ui(ids = manual_ids)
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  ptr_state <- ptr_server_state(
    "
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = var), size = num) +
  labs(title = text)
",
    ids = manual_ids
  )

  ptr_setup_controls(input, output, ptr_state, ids = manual_ids)
  ptr_register_draw(input, ptr_state, ids = manual_ids)
  ptr_register_export(output, ptr_state, ids = manual_ids)
  ptr_register_plot(output, ptr_state, ids = manual_ids)
  ptr_register_error(output, ptr_state, ids = manual_ids)
  ptr_register_code(output, ptr_state, ids = manual_ids)

  output$runtimeStatus <- renderText({
    runtime_result <- ptr_state$runtime()
    if (is.null(runtime_result)) {
      return("Waiting for draw")
    }

    if (isTRUE(runtime_result$ok)) {
      return(paste("Last draw succeeded:", runtime_result$code_text))
    }

    runtime_result$message
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Launch the app and confirm the embedded layout renders normally.
-   Before drawing, confirm the extra status output reads `Waiting for draw`.
-   Use `x = Sepal.Length`, `y = Sepal.Width`, `color = Species`, `size = 2`, `title = Embedded app test`.
-   Click `Update plot`.
-   Click `Export Shiny app`.

Expected checks:

-   The embedded app behaves the same as the default wrapper even with custom ids.
-   The extra `runtimeStatus` output can read `ptr_state$runtime()` without breaking the draw flow.
-   Plot, inline error area, generated code, and export button all still work through the bind helpers.

## Formula 11: Custom Plot Rendering with `ptr_extract_plot()`

This app checks the pure-value customization seam. It replaces the default plot
binder with a custom `renderPlot()` that adds a theme.

```{r formula-11-custom-plot, eval=FALSE}
ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Custom plot rendering"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      ptr_input_ui()
    ),
    mainPanel(
      plotOutput("outputPlot"),
      uiOutput("outputError"),
      verbatimTextOutput("outputCode")
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  ptr_state <- ptr_server_state(
    "
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = text)
"
  )

  ptr_setup_controls(input, output, ptr_state)
  ptr_register_draw(input, ptr_state)
  ptr_register_export(output, ptr_state)
  ptr_register_error(output, ptr_state)
  ptr_register_code(output, ptr_state)

  output$outputPlot <- renderPlot({
    plot_obj <- ptr_extract_plot(ptr_state$runtime())

    if (is.null(plot_obj)) {
      plot.new()
      return(invisible(NULL))
    }

    plot_obj + ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Launch the app.
-   Use `x = Sepal.Length`, `y = Sepal.Width`, `title = Custom theme test`.
-   Click `Update plot`.
-   Trigger one invalid input case, such as malformed `expr` if you add an `expr` layer, or use a missing-object formula variant if preferred.

Expected checks:

-   The rendered plot reflects the custom theme rather than the default ggplot appearance.
-   `ptr_extract_plot()` returns a plot only after a successful draw.
-   The blank-on-failure behavior still works because the custom `renderPlot()` handles `NULL`.
-   The default error and code binders continue to work alongside the custom plot renderer.

## Formula 12: Custom `date` Placeholder in a Live App

This app checks the new placeholder registry path end to end with a custom
`date` placeholder.

```{r formula-12-date-placeholder, eval=FALSE}
ptr_app(
  "
ggplot(data = sales_manual, aes(x = day, y = value)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = num) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = date, color = text) +
  labs(title = text)
",
  placeholders = date_placeholders
)
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Launch the app.
-   Confirm the custom placeholder control renders as a date picker.
-   Confirm its default label reads `Choose a date for xintercept`.
-   Use `size = 2`, `color = firebrick`, `title = Date placeholder test`.
-   Choose `2024-01-04` in the date input.
-   Click `Update plot`.
-   Change the date and draw again.

Expected checks:

-   The custom placeholder control renders alongside built-in `num` and `text` controls.
-   The plot includes a vertical line at the chosen date.
-   `outputCode` contains `as.Date("2024-01-04")` after the draw.
-   Changing the selected date updates both the plot and the generated code.

## Formula 13: Exported Custom Placeholder App

This check confirms that exported apps can rebuild a custom placeholder registry
and preserve custom placeholder copy overrides.

```{r formula-13-exported-date-placeholder, eval=FALSE}
date_formula <- "
ggplot(data = sales_manual, aes(x = day, y = value)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = date, color = text) +
  labs(title = text)
"

date_obj <- ptr_parse_formula(
  date_formula,
  placeholders = date_placeholders
)
date_out_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".R")

ptr_generate_shiny(
  date_obj,
  date_out_file,
  style = FALSE,
  ui_text = date_ui_text,
  placeholders = date_placeholders
)

date_out_file
source(date_out_file)
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Run the chunk and note the exported file path.
-   Inspect the exported script.
-   Confirm it defines `custom_placeholders <- ...`.
-   Confirm it rebuilds `placeholders <- ptr_merge_placeholders(custom_placeholders)`.
-   Confirm it defines `custom_ui_text <- ...` and rebuilds `ui_text`.
-   Confirm the exported server calls `ptr_server(..., ui_text = ui_text, placeholders = placeholders)`.
-   Run the exported app.
-   Confirm the date control label reads `Reference date`.
-   Confirm the date help text reads `Choose a cutoff date for the vertical guide.`
-   Choose a date, render the plot, and inspect the generated code.

Expected checks:

-   The exported app preserves the custom placeholder and the custom copy overrides.
-   The exported script serializes only the custom placeholder definition, not the built-in placeholder registry.
-   The exported app still renders the date-driven vertical line and generated code normally.

## Formula 14: Non-Inline Placeholder Export Failure

This check confirms the current exportability rule for custom placeholders.
Standalone exported apps require that each hook function is either defined
inline inside `ptr_define_placeholder(...)` or made available through a source
strategy (`source_function`, `source_file`, or `source_package`). Hooks that
are bare named references with no source strategy are rejected at export time.

```{r formula-14-non-inline-export, eval=FALSE}
build_date_ui_manual <- function(id, copy, meta, context) {
  shiny::dateInput(id, copy$label)
}

date_to_expr_manual <- function(value, meta, context) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(as.character(value), "")) {
    return(ptr_missing_expr())
  }

  rlang::expr(as.Date(!!as.character(value)))
}

non_inline_date_placeholder <- ptr_define_placeholder(
  keyword = "date",
  build_ui = build_date_ui_manual,
  resolve_expr = date_to_expr_manual
)

non_inline_obj <- ptr_parse_formula(
  "
ggplot(data = sales_manual, aes(x = day, y = value)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = date)
",
  placeholders = ptr_merge_placeholders(
    list(date = non_inline_date_placeholder)
  )
)

ptr_generate_shiny(
  non_inline_obj,
  tempfile(fileext = ".R"),
  style = FALSE,
  placeholders = non_inline_obj$placeholders
)
```

Suggested manual sequence:

-   Run the chunk.
-   Confirm `ptr_generate_shiny()` errors instead of writing a broken app.
-   Read the error message.

Expected checks:

-   The error explains that the custom placeholder must define `build_ui`
    inline or supply `source_file`, `source_package`, or `source_function` so
    exported apps stay standalone.
-   The package refuses the export before writing a standalone app that would be
    missing helper definitions.
-   This failure is limited to exportability; the same non-inline placeholder
    shape can still be useful for in-session experiments that are not exported.
-   Adding a source strategy (e.g. `source_function = list(build_ui = build_date_ui_manual, resolve_expr = date_to_expr_manual)`)
    to the `ptr_define_placeholder()` call would make the same placeholder exportable.

## Final Checklist

-   The manual sweep covers wrapper apps, upload flows, copy rules, export,
    embedded/bind-helper integrations, custom plot rendering, and custom
    placeholders.
-   Plot updates after clicking `draw`.
-   `outputCode` matches the active inputs.
-   Blank optional `text`, `num`, and `expr` values disappear cleanly from generated code where expected.
-   Unchecked layers disappear from both the plot and `outputCode`.
-   Unchecking every optional layer still leaves a valid base `ggplot(...)`
    render instead of a brittle runtime failure.
-   Upload-backed `var` controls appear only after valid upload.
-   Default upload naming works when the dataset name box is blank.
-   Custom upload naming works when a dataset name is provided.
-   Inline errors appear for malformed `expr`, bad upload types, and missing objects.
-   The plot clears and does not stay stale after an error.
-   Generated code remains visible when plot construction fails after completion succeeds.
-   Recovery after an error clears the error panel and restores normal plotting.
-   Exported apps run and preserve the same behavior for static and upload formulas.
-   Default copy-rule wording appears for shell text, upload prompts, numeric hints, and unnamed faceting prompts.
-   No default control label shows parser-style fallback text such as `argument 1`.
-   Custom `ui_text` override only the targeted prompts while untargeted controls stay on the defaults.
-   Exported custom-copy apps preserve the same wording as the live customized app while keeping only compact custom overrides in the script.
-   Embedded apps built from `ptr_server_state()` and the bind helpers still support custom outputs or observers around `ptr_state`.
-   Custom `renderPlot()` workflows can use `ptr_extract_plot()` and keep the rest of the default runtime behavior intact.
-   Custom placeholder controls render correctly, participate in code generation, and work alongside built-in placeholders.
-   Exported custom-placeholder apps rebuild `custom_placeholders` and preserve their custom copy text.
-   Export attempts for non-inline custom placeholders without a source strategy
    fail clearly instead of writing a broken standalone app.
-   Remaining internal-only or philosophy-boundary edge cases are reviewed in
    `tests/manual/ggpaintr-edge-cases.Rmd`.
